---
title: "Camera design framework"
subtitle: "Statistically robust two-tiered network for a coastal preserve"
description: "Technical report detailing a stratified, randomized camera network design — from research question development through placement maps and deployment logistics"
date: "2024-04-01"
# image: thumbnail-ltnc-cameras-2024.png
categories: [cameras, study design, statistical consulting, field protocol, report]
format: html
---

<!-- <a class="button-teal" href="https://github.com/morethangray/ltnc_cameras">Repository</a> -->

### Project overview 

This project designed a statistically robust wildlife camera network for the Jack and Laura Dangermond Preserve, working collaboratively with The Nature Conservancy to identify priority research themes and translate them into a rigorous monitoring structure.

### Objectives

- Identify key research themes and management questions to guide network design
- Develop a two-tiered camera network capturing both broad-scale and fine-scale data
- Ensure statistical robustness through stratification and randomization
- Provide camera placement maps and resources for deployment logistics
- Define methods for evaluating network performance and refining protocols over time

### Approach

A collaborative design process with the client grounded the network in actual management priorities. The two-tiered structure balances landscape-level coverage with targeted fine-scale monitoring. The final report includes a pilot study framework to assess network effectiveness before full deployment.

### Key findings / deliverables

- Complete camera network specifications with placement maps

- Stratified, randomized design to support detection probability estimation

- Data evaluation protocols and analysis recommendations

- Pilot study design for network calibration and refinement

### Citation

Gray M. 2024. Stratified camera designs for mammal monitoring: A framework for the Jack and Laura Dangermond Preserve. A technical report prepared by the Dwight Center of Conservation Science at Pepperwood, Santa Rosa, CA, for The Nature Conservancy. 46 pp.
